name: Timbale Cases
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the egg lightly and mix in milk, sugar, salt, and cooking oil.
2. Gradually sift flour into the mixture while continuously stirring until smooth.
3. Allow the batter to rest for 1 hour or until free of bubbles.
4. Heat a timbale iron by submerging it in deep hot fat (360°F).
5. Dip the heated iron into the batter, holding at an angle until the bottom is coated.
6. Lower the iron into the batter to within half an inch of the top.
7. Hold the iron straight and lower it fully into the hot fat (360°F).
8. Cook until the case is crisp and brown.
9. Remove from the fat; carefully release the case from the iron.
10. Drain on crumpled absorbent paper. If batter runs back into the bowl, the iron isn’t hot enough. If the case falls into the fat, it’s too hot.
